title: update_contact_fully single-transaction atomicity worth defending
description: Wrapping all field writes and the state bump in a single SQLite transaction is correct. The old code made separate store calls per field, each bumping state independently — a concurrent reader between the two bumps would see intermediate state, and the state token would advance twice for a single logical patch. The test test_contact_set_two_field_patch_bumps_state_once validates this invariant. A future contributor might suggest 'just loop over fields and call individual store methods' to simplify — that reintroduces the atomicity and state-bump bugs.
close_reason: Fixed in store.rs: panic for precondition, exhaustive-match test, SELECT 1 for existence check; decision records filed
